| **Bid Title:** | RFP 2026-05 Video Surveillance Camera Systems |

| **Status:** | Closed |

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Description: |
| # **\*\*Update to Bid Acceptance Date\*\***

## **Date has been extended to May 28, 2026 at 2:00 PM**

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | **City of Kemah**<br>**Notice to Bidders**<br>**Sealed Bid Project #2026-05**<br>**Video Surveillance Camera Systems** |  |

The City of Kemah is requesting proposals for the purchase of Video Surveillance Camera Systems for the Kemah Police Department.

Sealed Requests for Proposals (RFP) for **Video Surveillance Camera Systems**, Kemah Project No. **2026-05**, will be accepted until **2:00 PM on May 28, 2026**. Proposals must be submitted to the City Secretary, Natasha Hinton, either by email to **bids@kemahtx.gov** or delivered in person to **Kemah City Hall, 1401 Highway 146, Kemah, Texas 77565**.

All proposals must be received and date- and time-stamped prior to the stated deadline. Proposals received after this time will not be accepted. Proposals will be publicly opened and read aloud at **2:00 PM on May 28, 2026**, at Kemah City Hall.

Bidding documents may be obtained by contacting the City Secretary at **bids@kemahtx.gov** or by visiting the City’s bidding website at [**https://www.kemahtx.gov/bids.aspx**](https://www.kemahtx.gov/bids.aspx). There is no charge to view or download the documents.

PUBLISH DATES: Wednesday, May 6 ,2026

Wednesday, May 13 ,2026

\\_\\_\\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

### **Addendum \# 1- 05/18/26**

Quantity of Cameras: 20

Camera types: 5 PTZ, 10 dome, 5 multi-view cameras

**_Site Walk-Through:_** Is it possible to schedule an on-site walk-through to evaluate the proposed camera locations and existing infrastructure?

**This will be possible once a date has been selected.**

**_Camera Locations & Count:_** Is there a preliminary map of proposed camera locations, or an estimated minimum number of cameras the city is looking to deploy?

**The total number of cameras will be approximately 20, and they will vary. No maps will be shared at this time. Ten inside cameras and ten outside cameras. Please provide the cost of all accessories associated with the cameras, mounts, and adapters.**

**_Existing Cabling_**: Are vendors expected to pull entirely new cabling for the new system, or is the existing cabling infrastructure sufficient and available for reuse?

**At this time, existing cabling is standard Cat 5 and may be available for reuse, based upon equipment and system requirements.**

**_Network Equipment:_** Will the city be providing the necessary PoE switches and network infrastructure to support the new cameras, or should the vendor include network switching equipment in the bid?

**The vendor should provide quotes for all POE switches and other networking support.**

**_Storage Retention:_** The RFP mentions configurable retention policies and compliance with data privacy requirements. Does the city have a minimum required retention period (e.g., 30, 60, or 90 days) for video footage to ensure we quote the appropriate storage capacity?

**The Current policy is 60 days.**

**_Storage Preferences_**: The RFP mentions support for on-premises, cloud, or hybrid storage. Does the city have a preferred storage architecture, or should we propose what we believe is best? If cloud or hybrid is considered, what is the available outbound internet bandwidth at the primary site?

**Cloud-based is the preferred method of storage.**

**_Integration:_** Are there any specific existing systems (such as access control or specific identity management systems) that the new Video Management System (VMS) must integrate with from day one?

**If possible, Avigilon Alta is utilized for access control.**

\\_\\_\\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

### **Addendum \# 2- 05/20/26**

Site Visit/Walk-through:

Friday, May 22,2026 at 9:00 am. All perspective vendors will need to attend to successfully submit their bid. If any questions, please reach out to Capt. T.Ruiz at 409-204-2144 or [truiz@kemahtx.gov](https://www.kemahtx.gov/bids.aspx?bidID=19) and cc [bids@kemahtx.gov](https://www.kemahtx.gov/bids.aspx?bidID=19).

\\_\\_\\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

### **Addendum \# 3- 05/23/26**

### **\*\*Update to Bid Acceptance Date\*\***

### **Date has been extended to May 28, 2026 at 2:00 PM** |
